DISCOVERY 302, DEFENDANT HARI TAMANG MOTION TO COMPEL FURTHER DISCOVERY RESPONSES OF PLAINTIFF ABDULLAH ALSHARA. FORM INTERROGATORIES 8.1 - 8.5 AND 8.8 IS GRANTED. ALL OBJECTIONS ARE OVERRULED. THE COURT FINDS THAT THE INFORMATION SOUGHT MAY LEAD TO ADMISSIBLE EVIDENCE OF OTHER CLAIMS AND DEFENSES OTHER THAN WAGE LOSS. FORM INTERROGATORIES 8.6 AND 8.7 IS DENIED. SPECIAL INTERROGATORIES #40 AND #41 ARE GRANTED. ALL OBJECTIONS ARE OVERRULED. THE IDENTITY AND LOCATION OF WITNESSES IS DISCOVERABLE. CCP SECTION 2017.010. SPECIAL INTERROGATORIES #41 - 54 ARE GRANTED. ALL OBJECTIONS ARE OVERRULED. THE COURT FINDS THAT THE INFORMATION SOUGHT MAY LEAD TO ADMISSIBLE EVIDENCE OF OTHER CLAIMS AND DEFENSES OTHER THAN WAGE LOSS. REQUESTS FOR PRODUCTION OF DOCUMENTS #52 - 57 ARE GRANTED. ALL OBJEC TIONS ARE OVERRULED. THE COURT FINDS THAT THE INFORMATION SOUGHT MAY LEAD TO ADMISSIBLE EVIDENCE OF OTHER CLAIMS AND DEFENSES OTHER THAN WAGE LOSS. PLAINTIFF TO PROVIDE VERIFIED RESPONSES TO FORM INTERROGATORIES 8.1 - 8.5 AND 8.8, SPECIAL INTERROGATORIES #40 - 54, REQUESTS FOR PRODUCTION OF DOCUMENTS #52 - 57 AND DOCUMENTS TO REQUESTS FOR PRODUCTION OF DOCUMENTS #52 - 57 NO LATER THAN JANUARY 27, 2020. MOVING PARTY TO SUBMIT A PROPOSED ORDER THAT IS VERBATIM WITH THE COURT'S RULING AND IN COMPLIANCE WITH CRC 3.1312 VIA EMAIL TO KLGALLO@DISCOVERYREFEREE.COM. PROTECTIVE ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. JUDGE PRO TEM: KATHERINE GALLO, CLERK: S. LE, NOT REPORTED. =(302/JPT)

Motion To Compel Further Discovery Responses Of Plaintiff Abdullah Alshara
Off Calendar Jan-06-2020 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances:
Alexis Davidson, esq. (415-447-2833) of The Michael Law Firm for Plaintiff.
Natasha K. Zaslove, esq. (628-253-5073) of Tyson & Mendes for Defendants Uber Technologies Inc., Raiser-CA LLC, and Raiser LLC.
Julie Bratz, esq. (information not provided) of Bremer Whyte for Defendant Hari Tamang.

ARGUED. The Court having reviewed the pleadings and having heard oral argument rules as follows:

DEFENDANT HARI TAMANG Motion To Compel Further Discovery Responses Of Plaintiff Abdullah Alshara.

Form Interrogatories 8.1 - 8.5 and 8.8 is Granted. All objections are overruled. The court finds that the information sought may lead to admissible evidence of other claims and defenses other than wage loss. Form Interrogatories 8.6 and 8.7 is Denied.

Special Interrogatories #40 and #41 are Granted. All objections are overruled. The identity and location of witnesses is discoverable. CCP section 2017.010. Special Interrogatories #41 - 54 are granted. All objections are overruled. The court finds that the information sought may lead to admissible evidence of other claims and defenses other than wage loss.
Requests for Production of Documents #52 - 57 are Granted. All objections are overruled. The court finds that the information sought may lead to admissible evidence of other claims and defenses other than wage loss.

Plaintiff to provide verified responses to Form Interrogatories 8.1 - 8.5 and 8.8, Special Interrogatories #40 - 54, Requests for Production of Documents #52 - 57 and documents to Requests for Production of Documents #52 - 57 no later than January 27, 2020.

Moving party to submit a proposed order that is verbatim with the Court's ruling and in compliance with CRC 3.1312 via email to klgallo@discoveryreferee.com. Protective order signed in open court.

Judge Pro Tem: Katherine Gallo, Clerk: S. Le, Not reported. =(302/JPT)
